dpp_metadata.issuer_technology: IBM Granite 3-8B Instruct + NIR Spectroscopy (NIST methodology)
dpp_metadata.generation_method: Spectral analysis only — no QR code, no RFID, no embedded identifier
dpp_metadata.data_source: NIR spectroscopy + ML property prediction (NIST open data methodology)
dpp_metadata.identification_note: This passport was generated from spectral and physical property data alone. The item arrived at the recycling facility with no digital identity. MPIP created this passport from NIR measurements — covering the 91% of post-consumer plastic that carries no embedded identifier.

material_identity.identification_method: NIR spectroscopy + Random Forest property prediction

environmental_data.recycled_content_basis: Post-consumer recycled — verified by spectral analysis

circularity.recycling_pathway: Premium food-grade HDPE stream — PCR eligible for food-contact applications
circularity.end_of_life_instruction: Route to Premium food-grade HDPE stream — verified PCR grade A

traceability.sorting_technology: NIR spectroscopy + IBM Granite AI reasoning
traceability.reasoning_summary: Density of 0.9524 g/cm3 is within the HDPE range (0.941–0.965 g/cm3), confirming high-density polyethylene. Crystallinity of 0.6085 indicates well-ordered crystalline structure suitable for food-contact PCR applications. SCB of 15.6 CH3/1000C is consistent with commercial HDPE grades (typical range 2–12).
